During the Cold War, several leaders held power in key countries. In the United States, Presidents Truman, Eisenhower, Kennedy, Johnson, Nixon, Ford, Carter, Reagan, and Bush were in charge at different times. In the Soviet Union, leaders like Stalin, Khrushchev, Brezhnev, and Gorbachev took turns leading. These individuals shaped global politics and influenced the tense relationship between the two superpowers.
